description_long: This object is a hand tool with a robust, dark wooden handle and a solid, black metal head. The head is rectangular with a flat striking face on one side and a slightly tapered, wedge-like end on the opposite side, typical of a sledge or splitting axe. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made from a dense hardwood, showing a natural wood grain texture. The metal head is securely mounted atop the handle, with a visible metallic fastener or wedge at the top, ensuring a tight fit. The tool's utilitarian design suggests it is built for heavy-duty tasks such as splitting wood or demolition work. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing its functional, industrial character.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a straight wooden handle and a solid, rectangular black metal head, designed for splitting or striking tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight wooden handle and the rectangular black metal head with a flat striking face.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the narrow profile of the metal head and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle and the wedge-like profile of the head, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the metal head and the straight alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with visible grain, no curvature, smooth grip, and round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular, wedge-like black metal head with a flat striking face and a slightly tapered rear; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible fastener at the top of the handle.
